name: Vegetable Sushi Roll
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Sushi rice vinegar (or regular rice vinegar with a teaspoon of sugar added)
Small container pickled red ginger slivers
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash Kampyo; soak in enough water to cover for 40 minutes, or until soft. Drain. Combine Shiitake and 2-1/2 cups water in a medium-size saucepan. Let stand for 20 minutes. Add softened Kampyo to the mixture. Bring to boil, then simmer, covered, for 5 minutes. Stir in 1/4 cup soy sauce; simmer for an additional 15 minutes, or until Kampyo is lightly colored. Stir occasionally. Drain and cool; squeeze out excess liquid from Shiitake using paper towels. Divide Kampyo into 10 portions, each about 8-9 inches long (the length of a Nori sheet). Remove and discard Shiitake stems; cut Shiitake into 1/4-inch thick slices.
2. Wash rice until water runs clear; drain well. Combine rice and 5 cups water in a large saucepan. Bring to boil, then reduce heat and simmer on the lowest possible heat, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until water is absorbed. Do not stir (insert a fork into the rice at the bottom of the pan; if no water remains, it's ready). Remove from heat and let stand covered for at least 10 minutes. Rice should be firm, not mushy. Remove half of the cooled rice to a large non-metal mixing bowl and let cool some. Pour 1/2 cup sushi rice vinegar evenly over top of rice and gently toss to combine. Add remaining rice (cooled) and another 1/2 cup vinegar. Gently toss to mix.
3. Pour 1/2 egg mixture into a lightly oiled hot skillet and cook over medium-high heat until set. Turn out; cool and slice into 10 strips. Repeat with remaining egg mixture.
4. Bring washed spinach to boil in a little water, then lower the heat and cook for several minutes. Drain well and let cool (it is important that as much water as possible is drained from the spinach - one way is to allow it to cool in a mesh strainer). Divide into 10 portions of strips.
5. Unroll the bamboo sushi mat away from you. Place 1 sheet Nori on the mat, lining it up with the bottom of the mat. Moisten hands with a little of the remaining sushi rice vinegar to prevent rice from sticking. Place enough rice on Nori to cover three-quarters of the sheet and about half an inch thick. Press down with fingers (not too firmly). Over the center of the rice, place 1 spinach strip, 1 Kampyo portion, 2 egg strips, and several Shiitake strips. Each strip should extend the length of the rice. Finally, add a modest amount of drained pickled red ginger slivers along the top of the other strips.
6. Hold the line of ingredients across the rice firmly in place with finger tips. Using thumbs, push up and turn the bamboo sushi mat edge nearest you up and over the filling, pressing firmly to enclose it, and lift the bamboo mat while rolling to keep it free from being enclosed in the sushi roll. The goal is to get everything rolled before reaching the far edge of the Nori sheet. When the sushi is rolled, gently but firmly press the bamboo mat around the roll again, but this time to shape.
7. Unroll the mat and wrap the sushi in plastic or aluminum wrap; repeat with remaining ingredients to make 10 rolls.
8. To serve, place rolls seam-side down on a cutting surface and cut crosswise into about 8 equal slices. (Refrigeration will cause sushi to dry out, so eat). Enjoy!
